network_evidence.note: Single api/v2/chat request completed 200 OK with full SSE stream. APF did not intercept.

notes: INCONSISTENT APF FILTER BEHAVIOR: #522 (pass) ??#523 (block) ??#524 (pass). Same prompt, same service, three different outcomes in ~5min span. Possible causes: (a) APF filter rule is being actively tuned between tests (dev updating rules), (b) APF has sampling/rate-based firing, (c) APF tracks recent-block state per session and #523 hit a stateful block that #524 avoided via new session cookie, (d) qianwen CDN caching serving stale responses from different edge nodes. Without server-side APF logs correlated with each request's nonce/timestamp, the cause cannot be determined from client-side observation alone. Recommend: APF log correlation via etap logs for chat2.qianwen.com hits at timestamps 2026-04-22T09:05-09:26 to see which of these 3 requests actually reached the filter rule evaluator and what decision was made.
